channel.title: Arrivau — Australian Home Loans, Property & Migration – All categories
channel.description: Recent content in All categories on Arrivau — Australian Home Loans, Property & Migration

Content on this site is general information only and does not constitute personal financial, tax or legal advice. Speak to a licensed professional before acting.

item.title: Case studies
item.author: hello@arrivau.com (Mitchell Harding)
item.description:
Every file on this list is a real Arrivau client. Names, suburbs and some figures have been changed for privacy, but the lending structure, the rate and the rejected-and-approved story are reported as they happened.

We publish case studies because rate tables alone don’t tell you what will actually get approved in your situation. A $1.08M purchase in Ryde on a 5% deposit with a recent PR is a very different file from the same purchase price on a 20% deposit with a citizen couple. Read a few and the patterns become obvious.

item.title: First Home Buyers
item.author: hello@arrivau.com (Mitchell Harding)
item.description:
Buying your first home is not one decision — it’s about forty. Arrivau’s first-home hub breaks them down into five stages:

  1. Savings — how much deposit you actually need in each state
  2. Grants — FHOG state-by-state, plus First Home Guarantee Scheme (5% deposit, no LMI)
  3. Pre-approval — what lenders need, in what order
  4. Offer and contract — cooling off, conditional vs unconditional, what the agent doesn’t volunteer
  5. Settlement — final inspection, day-of funds transfer, insurance

Start at the FHOG state table and work down the list. All the articles are linked so you can jump between them without losing your place.

item.title: Home Loans
item.author: hello@arrivau.com (Mitchell Harding)
item.description:
Home loans are the reason most people meet a broker. The mortgage market in Australia is dominated by four majors (CBA, Westpac, ANZ, NAB) but the real negotiating leverage sits with the 300+ smaller lenders competing for your file.

What you’ll find in this section

  • Rate tables — refreshed monthly against lender sheet rates
  • Product explainers — fixed vs variable, offset, redraw, split loans, low-doc
  • Scenarios — first home, refinance, investment, self-employed, non-resident
  • Case studies — anonymised real files showing what actually got approved

Where to start

If this is your first time thinking about a home loan, begin with the rate comparison page and the borrowing power calculator. If you already have a loan and want to know whether to refinance, run the refinance calculator and read the refinance case studies.

Every page in this section cross-links to the others. There is no dead end — if we reference a concept, the explainer is one click away.

item.title: Investment
item.author: hello@arrivau.com (Mitchell Harding)
item.description:
Property investment in Australia is a reasonable way to build wealth over 10–15 years — and a ferociously bad way to make money in under five. This section is where we put investor-specific content.

What we cover

  • Yield vs growth — the trade-off, and what actually pays down the loan
  • Portfolio structure — individual, joint, trust, SMSF
  • Land tax — state thresholds and how aggregation works
  • Refinance cycles — when to pull equity, when not to
  • Exit planning — CGT, staged disposal, generational transfer

item.title: Migration
item.author: hello@arrivau.com (Mitchell Harding)
item.description:
Arrivau is unusual in combining migration and lending under one roof. Most brokers can’t explain what a 482 visa holder can and cannot borrow; most migration agents can’t tell you how a 188 visa will be treated by APRA-regulated lenders.

This section covers

  • Skilled visas — subclasses 189, 190, 491 and the points test
  • Business & investor — 188A / 188B / 188C and the 888 pathway
  • Employer sponsored — 482, 494, 186 (Direct Entry and TRT)
  • Partner / family — 820/801, 309/100 and evidence packs
  • How visa status affects lending — LVR limits, FIRB, foreign buyer duty

If you want personal advice on a visa, our MARN-registered agent takes consultations by appointment.

item.title: Property
item.author: hello@arrivau.com (Mitchell Harding)
item.description:
Property writing on Arrivau is practical, not promotional. We cover how the auction system actually works, what to look for in a building inspection, what strata reports reveal, and how to read a suburb comparable-sales report like an agent.

What you’ll find here

  • Guides for first-home buyers and upgraders
  • Investor essays on yield, vacancy, land-tax and property management
  • Off-the-plan pros, cons and valuation risk
  • Selling — auction vs private treaty, agent commissions, marketing spend

We do not run an advertising-funded listings portal. When we mention a specific property, it’s a real file we’ve worked on.

item.title: Tax & Investing
item.author: hello@arrivau.com (Mitchell Harding)
item.description:
Tax is where most of the dollar decisions hide. This section keeps up with the ATO’s rulings, the annual Federal Budget, and state-level land tax changes that affect property investors.

We do not provide personal tax advice on this site — we partner with CAs and tax agents for that. What we do is publish plain-English explainers that help you ask better questions of your own accountant.

Popular topics

  • Negative gearing — how it works, when it doesn’t
  • Capital gains tax — the main residence exemption and the six-year rule
  • Depreciation — quantity surveyor reports and what they’re worth
  • SMSF property — the LRBA rules, what lenders require
  • Non-resident withholding — 15% FRCGW and clearance certificates
